category: BCR-ABL / KIT / PDGFR inhibitor (tyrosine kinase inhibitor)
mechanism: Inhibits BCR-ABL, KIT, PDGFR and has reported inhibitory activity against FLT3, blocking oncogenic kinase signaling and AML cell proliferation
additional_notes: Active in several AML models including FLT3-mutated contexts; used as a comparator TKI in AML studies

mechanism: Inhibits SRC and ABL family kinases and other signaling kinases involved in AML survival and proliferation
additional_notes: Reported anti-proliferative activity in AML cell lines; multi-target TKI

mechanism: Inhibits RAF kinases as well as VEGFR, PDGFR and FLT3, thereby blocking proliferation and survival signaling in FLT3-ITD AML cells
additional_notes: Widely reported activity against FLT3-ITD–positive AML lines such as MOLM-13 and MV4-11; low-nM potency against FLT3

category: Multikinase inhibitor (FLT3, KIT, VEGFR, PDGFR, etc.)
mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, KIT, VEGFR, PDGFR and other receptor tyrosine kinases, suppressing growth-promoting signaling in AML
additional_notes: Known to inhibit FLT3 and suppress growth of FLT3-mutant AML lines

mechanism: Potently inhibits BCR-ABL and multiple additional kinases including FLT3, leading to strong suppression of FLT3-ITD–driven signaling
additional_notes: Strong FLT3-ITD inhibitor; highly potent in FLT3-ITD AML lines such as MOLM-13 and MV4-11

mechanism: Inhibits BCR-ABL, KIT and PDGFR tyrosine kinases, reducing downstream proliferative signaling
additional_notes: Less active in AML than newer TKIs; may be relevant in KIT/PDGFR-driven AML subsets and serves as a comparator drug

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and additional kinases, blocking FLT3-ITD–driven signaling and inducing apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Clinically used in FLT3-mutated AML; MOLM-13 is commonly used for midostaurin studies

mechanism: Selective type I FLT3 inhibitor that targets both active and inactive conformations, suppressing FLT3-ITD signaling and AML cell proliferation
additional_notes: Potent against FLT3-ITD AML lines including MOLM-13; often shows sub-50 nM IC50

mechanism: Highly potent type II inhibitor of FLT3, preferentially binding the inactive conformation to block FLT3-ITD signaling
additional_notes: Also known as AC220; very potent in FLT3-ITD–positive lines such as MOLM-13

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits Bruton's tyrosine kinase (BTK) and has off-target activity on additional kinases, perturbing signaling pathways relevant for AML cell survival
additional_notes: Primarily a BTK inhibitor but some AML lines exhibit sensitivity via off-target and signaling-network effects

mechanism: Selectively binds and inhibits anti-apoptotic protein BCL-2, promoting apoptosis of BCL-2–dependent AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as ABT-199; very active in AML including FLT3-ITD lines; MOLM-13 typically highly sensitive

mechanism: Inhibits anti-apoptotic proteins BCL-2 and BCL-XL, triggering mitochondrial apoptosis in leukemic cells
additional_notes: Also known as ABT-263; earlier-generation BH3 mimetic with anti-leukemic activity in multiple AML cell lines

category: ALDH inhibitor; proteasome- and NF-κB-modulating agent (with copper)
mechanism: Inhibits aldehyde dehydrogenase and, when complexed with Cu2+, interferes with proteasome function and NF-κB signaling, leading to AML cell death
additional_notes: Commonly tested with copper (e.g., 0.5–2 µM CuCl2) as a combination; proposed to target AML stem-like cells

category: Antidiabetic biguanide; AMPK activator / mitochondrial complex I modulator
mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial complex I and activates AMPK, inducing energetic stress and reducing AML cell proliferation at higher in vitro concentrations
additional_notes: Repurposed for anticancer effects; AML anti-proliferative activity typically observed in the millimolar range in vitro

mechanism: Raises lysosomal pH and blocks autophagic flux, leading to accumulation of damaged organelles and reduced AML cell survival
additional_notes: Often used interchangeably or in parallel with hydroxychloroquine for autophagy inhibition in AML studies

mechanism: Inhibits HMG-CoA reductase, reducing mevalonate pathway products and impairing prenylation-dependent signaling required for AML cell growth
additional_notes: Statin class; simvastatin shows similar AML growth inhibition; often tested as a representative statin

mechanism: Binds cereblon and alters ubiquitination of specific substrates, leading to complex immunomodulatory and direct anti-leukemic effects
additional_notes: Lenalidomide is a related IMiD with similar conceptual use; AML data are mixed but present

mechanism: Reversibly inhibits the 26S proteasome, leading to accumulation of misfolded proteins and inhibition of NF-κB signaling, triggering apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Clinically used in multiple myeloma; demonstrates strong pro-apoptotic effects in AML cell lines

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits the chymotrypsin-like activity of the proteasome, inducing proteotoxic stress and apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Next-generation proteasome inhibitor; effective in AML cell models including FLT3-mutant subsets

mechanism: Inhibits HDAC enzymes, leading to increased histone acetylation, altered gene expression, and induction of differentiation and apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as SAHA; widely used HDAC inhibitor with documented activity in multiple AML lines including MOLM-13
